global_production_variables.story_world_summary: Working-class Milwaukee present day. Nick is a feared, scarred older brother; Character_P (younger brother, given name withheld) lives with aging Ma in a fifth-floor Kirk Street walk-up, works pizza, and begins a strange connection with Sionna while Ma’s mind slips.
global_production_variables.tone: Gritty Midwestern realism with quiet mythic undertow (Viking pride, fate-like attraction); intimate VO-driven character study.
global_production_variables.era_and_place: Contemporary Milwaukee, Wisconsin; Kirk Street brick walk-up, neighborhood gym, pizza joint, Joe’s bar, public library.

Character_P.description: Adult male, late 20s–early 30s, Nick’s younger brother; ordinary working-class build, not flashy; lives with Ma; pizza-job clothes; introspective eyes; director label only—never a personal given name on screen until late-book reveal.
Character_P.voice_profile: Male, late 20s to early 30s, Midwestern Milwaukee working-class accent, medium pitch, conversational pace with reflective pauses when narrating, low-to-moderate energy, same voice every scene.
Character_P.name_reveal_note: Given name withheld until late book; on-screen and dialogue use only little brother / Nick’s brother / Character_P. No name tags, mail, ID, library card face, nurse call-out of given name, or spoken first name. Surname address in childhood flashback (e.g. teacher ‘Mr. Olsen’) is allowed as book text; still no given first name.

Character_Nick.description: Older brother; tall, muscular, imposing; face like a puzzle held with glue-gun scars—chin and over left eyebrow; reddish-brown messy hair as if he forgot to comb it; not ugly, rough from brawls; moves like people should clear a path; can look beet-red, prison-break rage, Viking bulk when angry; red fire in eyes, flared nostrils, clenched fists in rage; after Joe’s: reclusive, pox-marked face through door chain, obsessive revenge energy in filthy apartment; later chameleon-helpful with Ma including escorting her to mass.
Character_Nick.voice_profile: Male, early-to-mid 30s, deeper Midwestern baritone, confident clipped pace, commanding energy with occasional warmth for family, explosive when challenged, same voice every scene.

Character_Nick_Young.description: Nick at about age 11–12 (sixth grade): already tall tough kid, reddish-brown devilish-messy hair, smaller frame but same facial structure and early scar potential; schoolyard alpha; can muss little brother’s hair with rough affection.
Character_Nick_Young.voice_profile: Male child ~12, higher pitch than adult Nick, Midwestern, bold and protective energy, same young voice in all flashback scenes.

Character_P_Young.description: Character_P as a child ~8–10 (fourth grade / alley kickball era); smaller, ordinary kid clothes; watches Nick with pride and mixed love/hate; can laugh until tears; no given name on screen.
Character_P_Young.voice_profile: Male child, higher pitch, Midwestern, quieter than young Nick except when laughing hard, same child voice in flashbacks only.
Character_P_Young.name_reveal_note: Even as child, no given first name on screen; teacher may use surname form only if book-faithful.

Character_Ma.description: Aging mother; tired brown eyes, vision trouble, moves carefully around furniture; retired school janitor; often in house clothes; can look glassy-eyed when confused; may clutch chest when unwell; saggy puppy-eyed when scolding Nick’s swearing; little afghan around her when listening; can snivel and sob when realizing her senility; deer eyes when comforted; flustered about what to wear for mass.
Character_Ma.voice_profile: Female, elderly, softer Midwestern voice, slower pace, can thin or empty when confused, same voice every scene.

Character_Sionna.description: Young woman; short brown hair (sometimes pinned up regal); skinny but pretty; very pale skin; striking light blue eyes; wardrobe varies: flowered shirt and sandals on bedroom visit; yoga/campus casual; green suede sofa earlier continuity; chocolate-colored couch at duplex; moves with boneless smooth grace; philosophy/comparative religion student; hippie-parent background (born 1970, Woodstock pregnancy story); red eyes after pot; cotton fiber purse; can twirl brown hair over cheek; drives old Toyota Civic hatchback to parents.
Character_Sionna.voice_profile: Female, mid-20s, calm cool measured delivery, light Midwestern or soft neutral American, unhurried energy, same voice every scene.

Character_Gym_Guy.description: Adult male gym-goer at Moxie’s; mid-set on preacher curls; yields bench immediately when Nick approaches.
Character_Gym_Guy.voice_profile: Male adult, Midwestern, brief deferential energy if he speaks; same voice if reused.

Character_Mark_Conner.description: Neighborhood kid in alley kickball flashback; scared after ball goes through window.
Character_Mark_Conner.voice_profile: Male child, Midwestern, frightened energy in flashback only.

Character_Mrs_Engel.description: Older neighborhood woman; furious about kitchen window broken by kickball.
Character_Mrs_Engel.voice_profile: Female elderly, sharp angry Midwestern, flashback only.

Character_Librarian.description: Adult woman at library front desk; moving books; practical neighborhood-library look; thin little smile when checking out classics.
Character_Librarian.voice_profile: Female adult, Midwestern, helpful neutral desk energy, same if reused.

Character_Nick_Teen.description: Nick in high school years: taller teen version of Nick, reddish-brown hair, already intimidating; capable of midnight vandalism with lighter fluid.
Character_Nick_Teen.voice_profile: Male teen ~16–18, Midwestern, lower than child Nick but not full adult baritone, volatile angry energy, same teen voice in high-school flashbacks.

Character_Dr_Carson.description: Middle-aged male physician; has seen Ma for ~10 years; serious professional manner; office white coat or shirt-and-tie clinical look; discharge education on BP meds and stroke risk.
Character_Dr_Carson.voice_profile: Male, mid-40s to 50s, calm clinical Midwestern professional pace, serious when delivering bad news, same voice every scene.

Character_Nurse.description: Adult clinic/hospital nurse; practical scrubs; summons patients; helps with discharge teaching on blood-pressure meds and Nitro.
Character_Nurse.voice_profile: Female adult, Midwestern, efficient desk/clinic energy, same if reused.

Character_Dying_Soldier.description: Dream-only wounded man on a battlefield; blood at mouth; speaks one phrase before dying.
Character_Dying_Soldier.voice_profile: Male adult, strained dying whisper, brief; dream only.

Character_P_Soldier.description: Dream variant of Character_P as exhausted soldier in chaotic melee; period/armor-ambiguous battlefield kit; same face as Character_P; can be stabbed in chest by dream-Nick.
Character_P_Soldier.voice_profile: Same core as Character_P but strained, battlefield-shout energy in dream only; still no given name.
Character_P_Soldier.name_reveal_note: Dream soldier is Character_P; no given name.

Character_Bobby_Swenson.description: Fourth-grade boy; Character_P’s school friend; laughs until red-faced with dribble; muted and nervous when sixth-grade Nick appears.
Character_Bobby_Swenson.voice_profile: Male child ~9–10, Midwestern, high energy giggling that collapses into nervous quiet around Nick; flashback only; same voice if reused.

Character_Jennifer_Bales.description: Fourth-grade girl; brown braids halfway down her back with red bows that bounce when she walks; know-it-all stern face; forty-year-old stuck in a nine-year-old body energy.
Character_Jennifer_Bales.voice_profile: Female child ~9, Midwestern, prim know-it-all cadence; flashback only.

Character_Sue_Kid.description: Fourth-grade girl with Jennifer; stern face, shakes head at the boys; walks toward school building to report them; no detailed costume beyond ordinary late-80s/90s schoolgirl clothes matching era of flashback.
Character_Sue_Kid.voice_profile: Female child ~9, Midwestern, brief if any lines; flashback only.

Character_Mrs_Stark.description: Elementary teacher; glasses on beaded necklace resting on large chest; can put glasses on and tip head for head-to-toe appraisal; marches like a stiff old war-movie officer when done; end-of-year frayed patience.
Character_Mrs_Stark.voice_profile: Female adult, Midwestern teacher authority, clipped scolding pace, same voice in grade-school flashback only.

Character_Bob.description: Adult male friend of Nick; working-class Milwaukee hangout type; smokes from a ceramic pipe earlier; crude talk about girlfriends; Saturday-night bar crew; anticipates brawls; at Dairyland: flannel shirt untucked over small beer belly, hands in pockets, Marlboro and lighter from front shirt pocket; exaggerates thigh-slap laughs; spreads Joe’s-bar hero rumor about Character_P.
Character_Bob.voice_profile: Male adult, Midwestern, stoned casual pace, crude locker-room energy, same voice if reused.

Character_Sammy.description: Adult male friend of Nick; Bud-can drinker; joins mockery of girlfriends and ‘pussy whipped’ digs; Saturday-night pool/bar crew; dull watery eyes when drunk; wants Nick to set him up with Tonya.
Character_Sammy.voice_profile: Male adult, Midwestern, loud laughing energy, slightly stoned, same voice if reused.

Character_Tonya.description: Joe’s Bar waitress; blue jeans and white blouse uniform; brunette ponytail with ratted puffy bangs; nice shape; feisty ‘screw you’ look when ogled.
Character_Tonya.voice_profile: Female adult, Midwestern bar-server energy; brief if any lines; same if reused.

Character_Computer_Guy.description: Adult man selling a used computer; comes to Kirk apartment bedroom to set it up; ordinary working-class tech-adjacent look.
Character_Computer_Guy.voice_profile: Male adult, Midwestern, practical brief setup energy; same if reused.

Character_Campus_Librarian.description: Adult librarian or library staff on community-college campus; leads brief talk about reference resources.
Character_Campus_Librarian.voice_profile: Adult, Midwestern, instructional library-tour energy; same if reused.

Character_Skate_Kid_1.description: Teen ~13 on corner near hardware store; skateboard; laughs with friends at Character_P and Sionna.

Character_Skate_Kid_2.description: Teen ~13 with skateboard crew on hardware-store corner; joins the laugh.

Character_Skate_Kid_3.description: Teen ~13 third of skateboard trio outside hardware store.
Character_Skate_Kid_3.voice_profile: Male teen, Midwestern, brief if any lines.

Character_College_Students.voice_profile: Mixed adult Midwestern classroom murmur; same ensemble energy if reused.

Character_Lindsey.description: Nick’s ex-girlfriend; pretty; light blonde hair; brown eyes; pink sweater and boots on the bar night; once hopeful step-daughter figure for Ma; dumped ~a year ago for getting too into Nick’s personal life; can flatten against a wall to become invisible when violence looms.
Character_Lindsey.voice_profile: Female adult, Midwestern, careful nonchalant then annoyed then frightened energy; same voice if reused.

Character_Lindsey_BF.description: Lindsey’s new boyfriend at Joe’s; black wavy hair; dark eyes; Spanish or Italian look; caterpillar eyebrows that dip midline when suspicious/angry; protective arm around Lindsey; challenges Nick.
Character_Lindsey_BF.voice_profile: Male adult, Midwestern or lightly urban American, confrontational clipped energy in bar fight only; same if reused.

Character_Joe.description: Owner of Joe’s Bar and Grill; adult male; runs in with bouncer to restrain Nick when trouble brews.
Character_Joe.voice_profile: Male adult, Midwestern bar-owner authority; brief if any lines; same if reused.

Character_Bouncer.description: Big bouncer at Joe’s; tries to restrain Nick; gets head-butted so Nick can escape arm holds.
Character_Bouncer.voice_profile: Male adult, Midwestern, minimal dialogue; physical presence; same if reused.

Character_Sionna_Angel.description: Dream-only angelic form of Sionna: long brown hair, light blue eyes, long blowing white gown with long sleeves; rises from lake; dry while water swirls; conducts wind and water; same face as Character_Sionna.
Character_Sionna_Angel.voice_profile: Silent or soft dream presence; if any sound, same core as Sionna; dream only.

Character_Nick_Giant.voice_profile: No required dialogue; dream roar/presence only if needed; same if reused.

Character_Kevin_McCleary.description: Adult male regular at Moxie’s; strict diet bodybuilder; arms like the Hulk; short hair gelled and parted crisply on the side; small waist, muscled quads tight on pant legs; protein/vitamin know-it-all; ass-kisser who goes ga-ga over Nick’s lifts and spots heavy benches.
Character_Kevin_McCleary.voice_profile: Male adult, Midwestern gym-bro energy, excited rapid talk when gossiping, deferential about Nick; same voice if reused.

Character_Philosophy_Teacher.description: Middle-aged male community-college philosophy instructor; beer gut; classroom authority without polish.
Character_Philosophy_Teacher.voice_profile: Male middle-aged, Midwestern academic casual pace, lecturing energy; same if reused.

Character_Pie_Cafe_Staff.description: Brief counter person at the small pie-and-coffee restaurant two doors from the pizza place; practical diner look.
Character_Pie_Cafe_Staff.voice_profile: Adult Midwestern counter service; brief if any lines.

Character_English_Teacher.description: Female community-college English instructor; modern-fiction unit (Lolita, Slaughterhouse-Five); high-pitched cackling laugh when responding to student comments.
Character_English_Teacher.voice_profile: Female adult, Midwestern academic, high-pitched laugh, classroom lecture energy; same if reused.

Character_Dairyland_Staff.voice_profile: Adult Midwestern counter service; brief if any lines.

Character_Ginger.voice_profile: Female, older adult ~50s–60s, warm Midwestern-hippie soft pace, same voice every scene if/when on screen.

Character_Beehive_Grandma.voice_profile: Female elderly; crying/yelling memory presence; minimal or no clear dialogue required; flashback only.

Loc_Kirk_Apt.description: Downtown Milwaukee big brick walk-up apartment; modest working-class interior; Ma and Character_P’s home; also childhood bedroom continuity for Part Two night scene.

Loc_Kirk_Street_Exterior.description: Milwaukee downtown street; large brick apartment building; alley access behind.
Loc_Kirk_Street_Exterior.visual_lock: Big brick multi-story walk-up facade, urban Milwaukee street, working-class neighborhood signage, alley mouth with garbage cans behind building.

Loc_Kirk_Alley.description: Alley where kids play kickball; garbage-can-lid bases; Mrs. Engel’s kitchen window faces alley.
Loc_Kirk_Alley.visual_lock: Narrow brick alley, dented metal garbage cans and lids as bases, residential kitchen window low enough for a ball to smash through, Milwaukee overcast or late-day light.

Loc_Moxie_Gym.visual_lock: No-frills corner gym: free-weight area, preacher curl bench, chalk and iron smell suggested by plates and bars; patrons notice when Nick enters; 40–50 lb curl bars visible.

Loc_Pizza_Place.visual_lock: Modest pizza storefront, cement sidewalk lip, glass front door and large front window for staring out at sidewalk and park, warm interior light spill; working kitchen: ovens, dough, counters, cleaning; working-class Milwaukee strip; spring green outside when memory triggers; slow-night empty phone orders.

Loc_Joes_Bar.description: Local bar; Mondays football on TV; pool/darts; Nick and Character_P hangout; Saturday nights packed with working-class crowd; pool room separate from main bar.
Loc_Joes_Bar.visual_lock: Dim neighborhood bar and grill: TV on tall stack (often inaudible), bar stools, pool table with quarters stacked for games, people lounging with cue sticks, packed tables and standing drinkers, heavy rock music energy, waitresses in blue jeans and white blouses; Nick’s entrance parts attention; pool room can go pin-drop quiet before a fight; corner speakers for AC/DC; back kitchen path for staff warning Joe.

Loc_Street_Milwaukee.description: Cold sidewalks between apartment, Joe’s, library, bus stop, downtown coffee routes, doctor’s office approaches; night run routes with obstacles; strip between pizza place and pie cafe; routes north to Dairyland; Werner corner near Nick’s building.
Loc_Street_Milwaukee.visual_lock: Midwest city sidewalks, cold weather breath possible, library visible across street, ordinary cars and brick storefronts; grey low clouds; no sunny resort look; night: street obstacles, other pedestrians, cold air sting; spring green when weather warms.

Loc_Nick_TransAm.visual_lock: 1980 Pontiac Trans Am: burnt orange paint, yellow side flames, big eagle on hood, T-top, exhaust splitters; people look when it pulls up.

Loc_Ocean_Memory.visual_lock: Atlantic shore memory: sand under feet, incoming tide, wave sound and foam; brief lyrical contrast to Milwaukee grit; not a present-day location.

source_excerpts.excerpt: Big brother was mine. ... Nick was the guy everybody was scared of. ... His face looked like a puzzle with the pieces held together with sticky stuff from a glue gun.
We lived downtown in a big brick apartment building on Kirk Street. A fifth-floor walk-up...
when we went into Moxie’s, the gym down on the corner, one of those guys ... dropped that sucker right down on the ground and offered it up.

story_beats.intent: Open on Character_P’s claim of Nick as real Big Brother; establish Nick’s scarred, feared presence.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick walks into a Milwaukee bar or street frame; people notice and shift out of his way; close details of scars on chin and over left eyebrow, messy reddish-brown hair.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Big brother was mine. I’m not talking about that stupid book we had to read in junior high. No—my real Big Brother. Nick was the guy everybody was scared of.
must_not: any on-screen given name for Character_P
pretty-boy unscarred Nick

story_beats.intent: Home base: fifth-floor Kirk Street walk-up with Ma and Character_P.
story_beats.visual_event: Exterior big brick apartment building; cut inside modest fifth-floor apartment; Character_P and Ma in lived-in rooms; stairs imply walk-up pain.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I lived with him and Ma after dad took off when I was about eight. Downtown, Kirk Street. Fifth-floor walk-up—real pain in the ass to haul up the groceries.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Ma may be background in living room; Character_P near window or kitchen.

story_beats.intent: Nick’s strength myth—grocery bags like world’s strongest man.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick climbs stairs loaded with about six shopping bags in one trip; Character_P follows with the rest.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: When I went to the store with Nick, he could load up about six of those shopping bags and take them up in one trip—like world’s strongest man.

story_beats.intent: Moxie’s: gym guy yields preacher curls the moment Nick approaches.
story_beats.visual_event: Inside Moxie’s, a guy mid-set preacher curls a 40-pound bar; sees Nick moving toward him; drops the bar and offers the bench; Nick nods, takes over, switches to a 50-pound bar for warm-ups.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: One time at Moxie’s, a guy was doin’ preacher curls with a forty-pound bar. Mid-set he sees Nick, drops that sucker, offers it up. Nick just nodded. Had to have fifty for warm-ups.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Gym guy faces Nick’s approach; yields space; Character_P watches from side.
must_not: Nick politely waiting in line
fancy modern boutique gym

story_beats.intent: Brother loyalty under VO; Character_P won’t curl fifty.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P on lighter weights nearby; Nick owns the rack; brief look of trust between brothers.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I ain’t gonna curl no fifty pounds as a warm-up. That’s okay though. Nick is my brother. I know he’ll stand up for me no matter what.

story_beats.intent: Optional glint of Nick’s Trans Am as status symbol.
story_beats.visual_event: Burnt-orange 1980 Trans Am with yellow flames and hood eagle pulls up or sits at curb; heads turn.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Milwaukee is where we live. All our lives. Nick’s got a cool car—people always look up when he pulls up to the lights.

source_excerpts.excerpt: We used to play kick ball in the alley behind the building and set up bases with dented-in garbage can lids. One time little Mark Conner kicked a ball right through old Mrs. Engel’s kitchen.
Nick stepped right up to her and said he was the one who did it. ... Somehow Nick gave her cash to pay for it about a week later.

story_beats.intent: Mark terrified; young Nick claims responsibility to Mrs. Engel.

story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: She called Ma. Ma had to pay for the window. Somehow a twelve-year-old Nick came up with sixty bucks a week later. He never told Ma it was really Mark. None of us did. I was sure proud of him.

source_excerpts.excerpt: The thing about Nick was, he was a real-life Viking ... tall, strong people who survived in the middle of winter...
When we go downtown to the bars, Nick always goes in first to check out the place. The bouncers are even scared of him.
It’s a 1980 Pontiac Trans Am all decked out. ... sweet burnt orange with yellow flames on the side, and the big eagle still on the hood.

story_beats.intent: Viking ancestry pride over Nick’s physical presence.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick walks cold Milwaukee street like a warrior; optional brief symbolic cold/wind; Character_P watching with pride.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Nick was a real-life Viking. Ancestors from Norway or Sweden—Viking land—stayed in Wisconsin. Tall strong people who survived winter while others froze. Modern-day warrior.

story_beats.intent: Nick enters bar first; space clears; bouncers wary.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick pushes into a bar doorway first; patrons and bouncer register him; path opens; Character_P follows.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: When we go downtown to the bars, Nick always goes in first. Bouncers are even scared of him. He likes places where everybody is running out.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Nick leads; Character_P half a step behind.

story_beats.intent: Trans Am as crown jewel.
story_beats.visual_event: Detail pass on burnt orange paint, yellow flames, T-top, hood eagle; Nick driving or parked; Character_P riding shotgun smiling.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: 1980 Pontiac Trans Am—V8, power everything, T-top, exhaust splitters. Burnt orange with yellow flames and the big eagle on the hood. I love riding in that thing.

story_beats.intent: Factory money and nearby apartment; still cares for Ma.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick arrives at Kirk Apt to check on family; Ma in background; Character_P lets him in; sense he lives five blocks away but belongs here.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: He runs an assembly line at the metal factory—sometimes the big crane. Easy money for him. Cool apartment on Werner Street, five blocks away. But he comes over a lot. Feels like he still lives here.

source_excerpts.excerpt: “What’s Ma doin’ back in the bedroom at this time of day?” he says. ... “You got to keep an eye on her. She shouldn’t be sleeping in the middle of the day like that.
Ma worked for years being a janitor for the school system. ... Between her and me with my pizza job money, we make ends meet.
“You don’t need a degree to get a good job. ... You’re not gonna be a doctor or lawyer ... Hell, you’re too old anyway.”

story_beats.intent: Nick arrives and questions Ma in bedroom midday.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick in apartment doorway/living room; glances toward bedroom; Character_P present; Ma not visible or silhouette in bedroom.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Nick
story_beats.dialogue: What’s Ma doin’ back in the bedroom at this time of day?
story_beats.blocking_notes: Nick faces Character_P; eyeline toward bedroom then to brother.

story_beats.intent: Character_P shrugs; Nick lectures on old people going downhill.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P uncertain; Nick steps in, intense but familial; living-room space between them.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I don’t know, why?
story_beats.ambient_or_sfx: muffled TV from other room optional

story_beats.intent: Nick’s warning speech; Character_P accepts responsibility.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick gestures toward bedroom; Character_P nods, chastened.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Nick
story_beats.dialogue: You got to keep an eye on her. She shouldn’t be sleeping in the middle of the day like that. That’s how old people go downhill. They start sleepin’ all day, and pretty soon they don’t want to go outside, or eat, or go to the store or nothing.

story_beats.intent: VO: Ma’s hard life, janitor work, pizza-and-macaroni survival.
story_beats.visual_event: Inserts: Ma watching game shows/cooking channel bored; fridge with cheap food; Character_P in pizza-work shirt counting thin cash; Ma moves carefully, poor vision.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Ma worked years as a janitor for the school system—kids’ snot and puke. Back problems, retirement, social security. Between her and my pizza job we make ends meet. Lot of pizza and macaroni. Someone has to be here with Ma. She’s getting older. Hard for her to see.

story_beats.intent: School vs real world; Nick refuses factory help.
story_beats.visual_event: Brothers in kitchen/living room; Nick dismissive; Character_P frustrated but silent on the deeper hurt.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Nick
story_beats.dialogue: You don’t need a degree to get a good job. All you need is to know somebody. What kind of shit do you want to take at school anyway? Bunch of nerds talking to hear themselves talk. The real world is here—where we live. You’re not gonna be a doctor or lawyer. Hell, you’re too old anyway.
must_not: Nick offering the factory job

story_beats.intent: Character_P internal surrender; secret love of reading.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P alone after Nick leaves or turns away; quiet look toward a hidden paperback or just empty hands.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Nick is right. I am too old. Haven’t got the brains. Besides, I have to take care of Ma. I do like reading though. I don’t tell nobody that.

source_excerpts.excerpt: this girl tripped over the cement while she was walking toward me. She kind of fell into me. ... “Sionna” she said.
She said, “No thanks, I don’t eat pizza, but if you want to go have a cup of coffee sometime, you can call me.”
It was like I “knew” her or something. ... She moved real smooth. It was like there wasn’t any bones in her body. It reminded me of the waves...

story_beats.intent: Arrive at work; ordinary sidewalk before collision.

story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Something weird is happening to me. I can’t really tell Nick, or Ma, or Bobby down at the pizza joint.

story_beats.intent: Sionna trips on cement and brushes into him.
story_beats.visual_event: Sionna walking toward him trips on cement lip; falls/brushes shoulder into Character_P; she looks down and brushes off black V-neck shirt; fish-symbol necklace visible.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Are you okay?
story_beats.blocking_notes: Collision on sidewalk center; both face each other after; backpack on Sionna.

story_beats.intent: She says yes; first look; names.
story_beats.visual_event: She answers, brushes shirt; they look at each other not knowing what to say; he works here; she gives name Sionna.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Sionna
story_beats.dialogue: Yes.

story_beats.intent: Name exchange and Irish goddess / River Shannon (he barely listens—eyes).
story_beats.visual_event: Extreme close on her light blue eyes; lips move about name meaning; he is lost in eye color like pre-dawn sky.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I work here, I’m just going to work. What is your name?

story_beats.intent: Sionna names herself and origin; coffee invite instead of pizza.
story_beats.visual_event: Sionna speaks calmly; declines pizza; offers coffee sometime; he is freaked she initiated; she gives phone number (on paper or phone).
story_beats.speaker: Character_Sionna
story_beats.dialogue: Sionna. My mom and dad named me after an Irish goddess—something about the River Shannon. No thanks, I don’t eat pizza, but if you want to go have a cup of coffee sometime, you can call me.
story_beats.blocking_notes: She faces him; cool blue delivery; he takes number.

story_beats.intent: Uncanny recognition; she looks back over shoulder.
story_beats.visual_event: She walks away smooth, boneless; glances back over shoulder as if memorizing him; he stands fixed, weird feeling of being seen through.
story_beats.shot_scale_hint: medium long following her then on him
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: She was not cute—she was pretty. Pale like an old English story. When she walked away she looked back over her shoulder like she was trying to remember what I was going to look like tomorrow. It was like I knew her. Intense.

story_beats.intent: Ocean memory simile: tides covering his feet; he doesn’t want to move.
story_beats.visual_event: Brief flashback: Character_P as younger self feet in New Jersey sand, tide washing in; smash cut back to him on Milwaukee sidewalk watching her go.
story_beats.shot_scale_hint: lyric wide beach then return medium
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: She moved like the waves out by New Jersey—the only vacation I remember. Tides coming in… pretty soon you move or get covered. That was Sionna—except she was washing up on me, and I didn’t want to move my feet. I just wanted her to cover me completely.
must_not: extended vacation subplot
dad on screen if not needed

source_excerpts.excerpt: Ma was in a crazy mood. She was looking around the house mumbling to herself about trying to find her pain pills.
“Did we go to church today? Did you see Mrs. Canasta there?”
Her eyes were all glassy and looked really empty. It was like she didn’t know who I was or something.

story_beats.intent: Enter home; Ma searching and mumbling about pain pills.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P enters; Ma rummages counters/drawers; pill bottles already lined in a row on counter; she doesn’t register the system.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Ma, what’s the matter?

story_beats.intent: Ma’s confused questions: church, Mrs. Canasta.
story_beats.visual_event: Ma turns, lost; asks about church and Mrs. Canasta; Character_P stunned.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Ma
story_beats.dialogue: I can’t find my pain pills, I know I put them here somewhere. Did we go to church today? Did you see Mrs. Canasta there?
story_beats.blocking_notes: Ma faces Character_P but eyes unfocused.

story_beats.intent: He grounds reality; points to today’s pills.
story_beats.visual_event: He gently shows the lined bottles and today’s box right in front of her.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Ma, what are you talking about? We don’t go to church anymore. Mrs. Canasta lives down one level—all you have to do is walk down there if you want. Your pills are right in front of you in the box for today.

story_beats.intent: Glassy empty look—doesn’t know him—then snap back.
story_beats.visual_event: Close on Ma’s glassy empty eyes; beat of non-recognition; then she softens, apologizes, sees the pills.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Ma
story_beats.dialogue: Oh, right, I am so sorry. I see them right here. I don’t know what got into me.
must_not: comedic dementia gag

story_beats.intent: Character_P decides he must tell Nick; needs to escape apartment.
story_beats.visual_event: He watches her take a pill correctly; his face tight; grabs jacket to leave.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Wow. She was losing it. I would have to tell Nick. I’m sure he’ll get mad—like it was my fault. I had to get out of the apartment.

“Why don’t you take her to the store or something? ... Do you know how expensive that is?
He gave me that look like he wanted to punch me in the face. ... God, sometimes I really hate Nick inside.

story_beats.dialogue: I went down to Joe’s for a beer. Chances are I’d see Nick. Mondays he watched football. Made Mondays not seem so bad.

story_beats.speaker: Character_Nick

story_beats.intent: Nick’s nursing-home cost tirade and blame.

Holy Christ, do something with her before she turns into some demented old fart and we have to pay for a nursing home. I don’t have the money for that shit. They take every last dime out of these old people, then leave the family in debt after they die. Is that what you want to happen?

source_excerpts.excerpt: When I looked up, I saw the library across the street and just halfway down the block. I should go in there, I thought. Who’s gonna know anyway?
The windows were really big and the floors must have been wood from a hundred years ago. ... Old lights hung down on metal chains from the ceiling
“How do I take a book home from here?” I asked the lady at the desk.

story_beats.dialogue: It was freakin’ cold. I walked faster. Then I saw the library across the street. Who’s gonna know? Ma can barely remember what’s going on. Nick won’t know.

“It’s so cold outside. I don’t have a nice coat to wear. Where are we going anyway?”
If Nick wants her to go outside so bad, then he can drag his ass over here and get her to go.
I swear sometimes, there’s no reason for me being born anyway, except to have somebody for Nick to boss around.

“Hi, Sionna? This is the guy that got your number the other day outside of the pizzeria. Do you remember me?”

source_excerpts.excerpt: I walked in, and saw her near the wall in the back at a small table. She had a backpack too.
All I saw were those eyes, they were like a watery blue crayon.
“What are you reading right now?” she asked. … “Wuthering Heights. … ” “Yea, I know. I read it.”
A love that never changes will destroy you. It reminded me of the way Nick is; strong and unwavering, but somehow it seemed dumb.
We made another date to go to dinner on Friday night. I couldn’t wait.

story_beats.intent: Enter Java Hut: college magazine crowd; he feels out of place.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P enters; backpacks, textbooks, groups talking school, glasses on heads; he scans, awkward.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Everybody looked right out of a college magazine. Backpacks, textbooks, notes—too cool for me. Until now.

story_beats.intent: Sees Sionna at back wall; eyes erase everything else.
story_beats.visual_event: Sionna at small back table, backpack, green hooded top; he approaches; close on her watery blue eyes; time seems to stop.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I didn’t see the jeans or the green top. All I saw were those eyes—like a watery blue crayon. I lost all track of time. She really was beautiful.
story_beats.blocking_notes: He sits opposite her; mutual eyeline across small table.

story_beats.intent: School talk; he feels dumb; she has English degree.
story_beats.visual_event: Conversation; she calm and articulate; he shifts, self-conscious.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: You ever go to school?
story_beats.ambient_or_sfx: Sionna: degree in English—loves to read and write stories; history and a million other things (compressed).

story_beats.intent: What are you reading—Wuthering Heights; she already knows it.

story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Wuthering Heights. It’s the story of this guy and girl and how they grew apart but were really in love with each other.
story_beats.ambient_or_sfx: Sionna: Yea, I know. I read it.

story_beats.intent: She teaches symbols: ghosts, doubles, moors, destructive unchanging love.
story_beats.visual_event: Animated talk montage within scene: her hands, his rapt face, coffee cooling; intellectual electricity.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Sionna
story_beats.dialogue: The moors are like the people—wild, unpredictable, infertile. Heathcliff’s love for Catherine never changes… and that kind of love destroys him. A love that never changes will destroy you.

story_beats.intent: Internal link to Nick; something waking in him.
story_beats.visual_event: His eyes go distant then return to her; lean-in energy.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: It reminded me of Nick—strong and unwavering, but somehow dumb. Would it destroy him too? Something inside me was coming awake. I could talk to her about anything. She made me feel smarter just by being around her.

story_beats.intent: Friday dinner date set; he can’t wait.

story_beats.speaker: Character_Sionna
story_beats.dialogue: Friday night—dinner.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Parting at table/door; warm but not kiss yet.

source_excerpts.excerpt: Maybe that was Ma’s problem all along. Dad left, and Ma never moved on. It just ended up destroying her.
Well, I knew one thing – there aint no way I could ever forgive him. He is the lowest of the low.
Let’s face it, Nick became my dad when I was eight.
I make a good pizza because I do it every day. … the more you practice being a good parent, the better the kids will be in the end.

story_beats.intent: Leave café; love that can kill if you don’t let go.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P walks down street toward bus stop; afterglow face turning thoughtful.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I thought again about love that can end up killing you if you don’t let it go where it’s supposed to.

story_beats.intent: Ma destroyed by never moving on; would still forgive Dad.
story_beats.visual_event: His walk slows; city grit around him; memory weight without showing Dad.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Maybe that was Ma’s problem. Dad left and she never moved on. Even if he came back today she’d forgive the pain, the money, missing baseball games and graduations. All she wants is that same guy at that same time.

story_beats.intent: He will never forgive; Nick became dad; shame of his place in life.
story_beats.visual_event: Harder jaw; bus stop comes into view; he waits.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: There ain’t no way I could ever forgive him. Lowest of the low. Left us to raise ourselves. That’s why I’m here—no college, no decent job, and I have to listen to Nick. Nick became my dad when I was eight. It’s a damn shame.

story_beats.intent: Creed: never leave; practice parenthood like pizza and weights.
story_beats.visual_event: Bus approaches or he boards; resolve in eyes.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I would never do that. A guy’s duty is to stand up for his family. Practice makes perfect. I make a good pizza because I do it every day. Maybe there’s so many shitty people because nobody’s practicing being a parent.

I am sitting very close behind her, holding on around her waist. Occasionally, I lean forward to place my face next to her neck… mix of rose and warm grass.
I reach for her, and lift her gently as she drops down from the trusty dappled mare… I kiss her, and somehow I know this is the first kiss we have shared.
I look into her eyes, and they are the lightest blue, like the early morning light.

source_excerpts.excerpt: “Hold!” I screamed, as I bolted upright in bed. My stomach was tight, my chest pounding like a drum. I was sweating, even though it was cold in my room.
It was Sionna, but in a different place with old clothes on. What is happening? Did I insert these people from life into some kind of Wuthering Heights book?

story_beats.speaker: Character_P

source_excerpts.excerpt: I have started to find that some people are beautiful inside as well as out. Sionna is one of those people.
I told her about my crazy dream. She just smiled and looked through me with those watery eyes.
“Did you know that Buddhists consider everything around us to be a dream?” she said.
“Maybe you were dreaming about something from a past life” she said.
She made me feel like I belonged somewhere; like I really mattered.

source_excerpts.excerpt: I called her up and told her I wished she would finally come over to my place, and even meet my Ma. She wasn’t really surprised, just a little hesitant.
“Are you sure? It’s kind of a big deal to meet your mother.”
“What I really need to talk to you about is some stuff I read in a book about Buddhism.”
“You’ve already tried it silly… when we just lie there at the end – in Savasana, or corpse pose.”
I was starting to feel like a real person… a person by myself - without Nick, without Ma, without my job and apartment and belongings.

The nurse told me I should bring her in that afternoon… we had to ride the bus about three miles to the office.
I get so sick of doing this shit. I didn’t sign up to be a nurse, but it sure feels like I am.

source_excerpts.excerpt: It turned out they only kept Ma in the hospital for three days. Nick tried to come when he was off work, but I was there pretty much all of the time
Sionna met me at the hospital one day for lunch and we walked outside and around the neighborhood.
Her eyes sparkled like diamonds… light blue diamonds… Excited because I couldn’t wait to see her again… Calm because she had this way of talking to me that made me feel really special
Actually, I think I loved her, I just hadn’t told her yet.
He tried to talk, but the only word that came out was “Save her” and blood bubbled out of his mouth… Nick was running towards me.
“Nick! Nick! Thank God you’re here! I thought I was going to die.”

source_excerpts.excerpt: “That you are little brother” he hissed at me, and then suddenly stabbed the longest knife I have ever imagined into my chest.
I couldn’t believe it. Nick had killed me – Nick had killed me.
I woke up gasping for breath; my heart pounding in my chest. I was still at the hospital. Ma was dozing off in her bed.
It was the feeling of betrayal by my closest family member. That had to be the worst betrayal of all.

source_excerpts.excerpt: I didn’t have much time to think about this weird dream, because I was getting Ma all set up to come home.
The nurses and Dr. Carson spent a lot of time with me, making sure I knew how important it was for Ma to stay on her blood pressure medications.
She also had some pills called Nitro, that she could use in case she had chest pain.
She also had a little bit of what they call “dementia”, or as far as I can describe it, just old people’s brain.
Sionna went with me to the drug store and we bought a plastic container labeled with all the days of the week, Sunday through Saturday.
For the first time in my life, things were starting to go right. I was going to be responsible for keeping Ma alive.
As far as Nick goes, that one I’m not so sure about. He hadn’t met Sionna yet, and I was in no big hurry for that.

source_excerpts.excerpt: Later that night, while Sionna went to her philosophy class, I got Ma all tucked in, explained to her the pill routine, and showed her the container.
“See Ma, right here is every day of the week, and every day will have the pills in in you need. There’s even separations for morning and night”
“Oh my Lord, that looks a little overwhelming” she said eyeing the plastic blue container suspiciously.
“You’re such a good boy. You and Nick are like a God-send to me. What would I do without you two?”
I had to think to myself. “You two?” Like Nick was ever around anymore to take care of anything.

“What’s up little Bro? You got Ma all tucked in? Man, you shoulda been a nurse!” he laughed so loud I thought he would wake Ma up.
“Oh, holy shit! Aren’t you nurse Nancy! Well, well, how did you come up with this little idea anyway?”
“If you must know, Sionna helped me figure it out. She’s really smart and good at that shit.
“This chick sure has you wrapped around her finger. I mean, if you must know.”
“Don’t you talk to me like that, you little bastard.”
“How the hell do you know? Maybe you are. You sure are a giant pussy – and that makes you and me way different Bro.”
Whack! Nicks hand slapped me across the head. My left ear was ringing, and I suddenly felt very sick.
In that split second, I knew I never wanted Sionna to meet that prick.

source_excerpts.excerpt: It was the most perfect spring day. We were at the tail-end of the school year with only a week before summer break. The lilac bushes were blooming at the back edge of the school property.
“You guys are in so much trouble when Mrs. Stark finds out you’re making fun of her” Jennifer said with her know-it-all voice.
“You HAVE to learn some responsibility!” I cried, barely able to spit the words out through the raucous laughter.
“What are you two numbskulls doin’?” he asked looking around for a clue.
“Mr. Olsen! Mr. Olsen!” she started declaring as she got closer.
“Um, sorry Mrs. Stark” Nick interrupted. “He didn’t mean anything by it. He and Bobby were just goofing around.
“Well, Nick, I know you’re a good student. It’s too bad your brother here doesn’t have the same level head as you.
It made me love him. It made me hate him. If they only knew the kind of stuff he did.
It was like he was magic or something.

source_excerpts.excerpt: I found myself staring out the front window of the pizza parlor. The grass was turning green, and the weather was getting warmer. That must have been what triggered the memory of Nick and me in grade school.
He had always been the alpha dog of the family… I don’t think Sionna really got the full gist of this. She hadn’t met Nick yet.
They were both the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end – of me anyway.
A couple months had gone by since my fight with Nick. He was now avoiding us like the plague.
Since I had started meditation, and been doing it on a regular basis, things weren’t so critical.
I could see that a lot of what Nick once meant to me was kind of silly, in a way… Nick was just a person.
The best part was, she really loved Sionna… To actually feel like I was being valued for a decision that I had made, not that Nick made… felt awesome.

source_excerpts.excerpt: The music was turned up loud as we opened the door to Joe’s Bar and Grill. People were lounging along the wall… Quarters were stacked up on the sides of the pool table
This was the place where people like us came on Saturday nights… the working poor and middle class.
it felt so – dumb. Just dumb… Oh well, when in Rome, do as the Romans do.
Nick ordered a Jack and Coke, Sammy and Bob had BPR’s, and I had a Bud.
“That Tonya, she’s a feisty one.” … “You want a date with her?”

source_excerpts.excerpt: “What’s up Lindsey” Nick asked her as he approached. She was wearing a pink sweater and boots.
The guy she was with came up next to her and put his arm around her in a protective sort of way. He had black wavy hair and dark eyes.
“What the fuck is wrong with you?” the dark haired man said. “You got a problem with me? Hell, I never even seen you before, and you’re in my face like a little bitch!”
Red fire came out of Nick’s eyes. His nostrils flared, and he had his fists clenched.
“…so join me for a drink boys….we gonna make a big noise…have a drink on me….have a drink on me…”
I could see Nick’s creased brow, his mouth twisted into a weird shape, and his right fist slowly drawing back and moving toward my face.
Joe and his big bouncer came running into the room and they tried to restrain Nick.

I ran, and ran, until my chest felt like it was about to explode. My ears were reverberating with the sound of my own heartbeat
I thought I was running home, but I found myself running to Sionna’s house, into the arms of the only person that really understood me.

source_excerpts.excerpt: “Your brother tried to hit you.” “Yes, he did.” “No one does that, you know. I mean, no one who really cares about you.”
“I think he is a controlling ass, and you are showing him that you don’t need him. You are the voice of reason, and he doesn’t understand reason.”
“You have been to yoga, and meditation now for half a year. Your mind is starting to work differently.”
“My thought is that we really won’t know until life is over.” “Um, isn’t that a little too late?” I started laughing.
“Sionna, the first time I met you, I felt so comfortable. It was like, I don’t know how to explain it, but, it was like, I had finally come home.”
“Me too honey. Me too.” … “By the way – the purpose of your life is to find the true self within you, and to honor it.”

source_excerpts.excerpt: The world was in black and white. I was the same man I had always been, but felt exhausted, as if I had just been in a three day battle and somehow survived.
From the middle of the lake she rose. It was a slow motion dance with wind and water swirling around her. I could see her face, long brown hair, and eyes of light blue.
The world suddenly had color. Vibrant greens, blues, and yellows shocked me out of semi-consciousness.
Nick emerged from the woods to our right. He appeared as a giant, with large teeth and eyes of fire. He held a weapon. It was a mace he whirled above his head
She spread out her arms with her sleeves blowing out, making her appear to be flying. Nick was being pushed back with an invisible force.

I glanced up at the kitchen clock to see that it was three a.m.
“I had the craziest dream. You were an angel, and came out of the water. You protected us from Nick with some kind of magical force!”
She started to giggle. “Well, that sounds about right.”
We shuffled down the dark hallway, peeled off our clothes and climbed into the crisp fragrant sheets of her queen-size bed.

source_excerpts.excerpt: The next months seemed to drag on, with school, work, Ma, Sionna, and all of my homework pulling the last of my energy. I was doing well at school.
I had to constantly re-evaluate who I once was, and who I was now. What I mean is, that I could see myself growing.
Nick was completely avoiding us, and Ma was confused. She couldn’t figure out why he never came around.
When she asked me where he had been, I would cover for him, telling her he had a poker game, or was at the gym or something.
Sionna made this outrageous casserole with butternut squash, cheese, and crunchy nuts.
Even if she forgot what was said, she remembered who said it, and the feeling behind it

source_excerpts.excerpt: I knew something had to give, but there was nothing to give. Everything mattered. Everything was important, and I had to push through it.
I also knew Sionna was watching me. She was observing how I dealt with stress, and I wanted to make her proud of me.
I wanted Nick to feel proud of me too. What a crazy thing. I would probably still be trying to prove myself to Nick, even if I turned ninety.
The teacher, who was a middle aged guy with a beer gut, told us that it is the science of “thinking about thinking”
Socrates said, “The unexamined life is not worth living.” I was starting to believe this.

source_excerpts.excerpt: I was floundering along when the doorbell rang. I hadn’t expected to see Sionna for another couple of hours, but there she stood in all her beauty in my doorway.
She flopped down on my bed with an exhausted sigh. “Geez, what a day. I have been running around like a chicken with its head cut off.”
“I love you.” I said when she finished.
“So, what’s your paper about?” “Oh, it’s not really a big paper. It’s just some thoughts on dualism and Descartes.”
“Well, I was born in 1970, and my mom always said she got pregnant with me at Woodstock.”
“Sionna is a Celtic Goddess, really it’s pronounced like ‘Shanna’… Matron of the river Shannon… possessor of wisdom”
“Okay hun, I will do your dirty work, and get in touch with you later. I have study group in a half hour.”

source_excerpts.excerpt: The night went so slowly, there were hardly any orders phoned in. It was nice outside, and I just stared out at the sidewalk and the park across the street.
on break I headed two doors down to a small restaurant where a couple people were inside eating pie and drinking coffee. I ordered a large black coffee to go.
when I looked up, there was Kevin McCleary right in front of me. He was one of the regulars down at Moxie’s.
“Man, I heard what happened at Joe’s. What the hell?” … “They said you hauled off and clocked Nick… People at the gym were all talkin’ about it.”
“Look, I don’t know who told you that, but it totally isn’t the truth. Actually, Nick almost kicked my ass… Please – tell everybody it wasn’t like that.”

source_excerpts.excerpt: Later that night I called Sionna and told her what happened. She just asked me how I felt about it, and I told her the truth. She said that was not productive.
I should be capable of being the hero of the story for once, even if it isn’t true. I should look at it as an opportunity for Nick to eat some humble pie
as I brushed my teeth and looked at myself in the mirror, I heard her voice in my head. I had to face the truth – I wasn’t the same shadow of the boy I once was. I had to grow some balls.
I will have to deal with the consequences from Nick someday. I hoped that someday would never come.

source_excerpts.excerpt: After class the next day, I got to talk to Sionna quickly at lunchtime. We were planning our big afternoon with the weed she had acquired.
I saw her sitting on the front porch of her house as I walked up. The sun was out, but there was a hint of rain to come.
The house was an old duplex. She rented the right side as you faced the front.
We sat on the old, lumpy, chocolate colored couch.
She opened the lid and retrieved a small, four inch long blue ceramic pipe with black swirly designs on it.
She lit the bowl and the green leaves shriveled and popped as she sucked in the smoke… “Wow! That is pretty strong… Here, you go.”
The fragrant dark smell of it felt nostalgic in some way. It was like I was transported back in time. I felt like a kid again.

source_excerpts.excerpt: smoke hit my lungs like a blast off a flame-thrower. I tried to keep it in, but the smoke rushed out in a lurch. I just laughed and coughed some more.
“Yowsa! You’re right. I don’t need much of that either.” We just sat back and rested a couple of minutes, then she walked over to put in a CD by Green Day.
“I think it just means that none of us knows what will happen in life, but we have to make the best of things. In the end, I guess we’ll see that everything is alright.”
She told me she could sometimes see “people’s auras.”
“No, I can’t see it right now. But I can say that once or twice I saw it, and you are a beautiful color of blue, like light blue.” … “You’re a loyal person.”
“You have mystified me! Let’s go outside, it’s too nice to sit here on the couch.” … “Ice cream! We must have ice cream!” she wailed.

source_excerpts.excerpt: We hit the sidewalk, and headed north to the Dairyland on the corner. There were only about three people hanging around the tables in front.
Suddenly, I felt conspicuous, as if the house held protection, but out here in the world, it was just us. Two stoned people trying to communicate to an unlike species.
Somehow I managed to order a soft serve chocolate in a waffle cone and found myself sitting with her on one of the curved cement benches around a sticky white table.
I looked up, and there in front of me stood Bob, Nick’s buddy. “Wow, man! How are you?” … “Haven’t seen you since the big brawl at Joe’s.”
“Hey, Bob, this is my girlfriend Sionna. Sionna, this is Bob.”
“Did you know your boyfriend is famous?” … “Your boyfriend here single-handedly conquered the BEAST man!”
“Man, you were right in Nick’s face. You moved like Bruce Lee, dog! … Everybody is talking about it.”
“No, man. I haven’t seen or heard from him since that night. I thought he would be at your Ma’s or something. You know, begging to the new king of the shit hill!”
“He’s NOT my friend. He’s Nick’s friend, I just got stuck hanging out with him, that’s all.”

source_excerpts.excerpt: Tough times went slowly. Ma was not usually a handful, but with all the other stuff on my plate, she was becoming one.
Nick was never around, it’s like he disappeared off the face of the earth.
This morning she was in the chair when I got up. “I smell something burning, can you smell it?”
the kitchen smelled like burning metal. The old coffeepot had boiled dry and the metal was starting to smell.
“Ma, how many times do I have to tell you NOT to try to cook anything? You’re going to burn the damn building down!”
“I can’t remember anything anymore. I’m no good for anything.”
“Nick doesn’t love me. He never comes to see me anymore. What’s wrong with him?”
“Just make sure Nick is alright. … My heart is a mess already. I feel it pounding a lot.”

source_excerpts.excerpt: I had a tough time in English class that day. I was supposed to be concentrating on modern fiction, but my mind was drifting back to last night.
I could feel the soft linen sheets, and Sionna’s skin. … The remembered smell of lavender and citrus triggered my brain into a soft, calm space.
My head actually began to nod. I quickly snapped back into place with the high-pitched cackle of my teachers laugh.

“Yes, John, many people think this is not a novel about a disturbed adult corrupting a child, but a novel about a disturbed child corrupting an adult! Hahahahaa! Well, that’s it for today folks. On Thursday let’s talk more about this, and please start reading “Slaughterhouse-Five”.
I scooped up my books and glanced at the clock. It was only 3:45, so I had to check on Nick.

what an ass. I can’t believe what an ass Nick is. … He once was the center of my world, and now he was just an annoying side-note.

source_excerpts.excerpt: I tapped lightly at first, “Nick, Nick?” After getting no response, I knocked a little louder.
The old door creaked open just a slit; the tarnished gold chain on the other side still attached. I could see the crazy brown hair and pox-marked face on the other side.
“What do YOU want?” Nick said accusingly.
“I really don’t need to talk to you man. Why don’t you just go home? Haven’t you done enough damage?”
The living room looked like a damn tornado had gone through. Papers, trash, and empty food containers lined the coffee table and side table.
“You’ve ruined my life! I can’t go anywhere, Man. People are talkin’ shit about me. I can’t go to the gym. … They’re sayin’ all kinds of crap, like I’m a pussy, my little brother tried to kick my ass
“Nick, man, whoa. Slow down. I didn’t do anything, man.” I used a slow, soft voice. It felt like I was trying to tame a wild dog.
“I truly am sorry if you think I did something wrong. … I just wanted to hang out, you know? Just hang out and enjoy the night.”
“You…YOU…are not the brother I knew, man. You’re not! All I know is now you’re some hot shit college kid with a hoity-toity girlfriend, and you know everything. Everything!”

source_excerpts.excerpt: I had to stand up to his accusations. “Nick, I’m not like that. I’m not. You just need to come over and see Ma and me more often. If you met Sionna, I know you would like her.
That’s the thing about going to school; you find out how much there is out there that you don’t know.” I started to hang my head and look at my hands folded in front of me.
I felt like a man on trial, waiting to hear if I had life without parole, or the death sentence.

“I don’t really give a shit how hard you’re working. I don’t give two shits about your girlfriend, and I don’t give three shits about how dumb you are. But here is what I have to say. You are my little brother. I do care about you and Ma, and I will let it slide this time. But don’t you ever, EVER, interrupt me in public again. You got that?”
“So, will you see Ma? She’s dyin’ to see you, man.” … “Yeah, I’ll come over there tomorrow. Tell her I’ve been sick or somethin’. Don’t tell her about any of this shit.

The door shut, and on the other side, I heard him rustling around in the papers, and dishes. I hoped he was picking them up, but maybe he was just rearranging the mess to lay down. I went on home to check on Ma.

Yes, I am trying to dig my way out of my stupid job. But that’s all, man. I don’t know everything. I hardly know anything. That’s the thing about going to school—you find out how much there is out there that you don’t know. I’m working my ass off, and finding out how stupid I am. That’s it, man.

source_excerpts.excerpt: Ma was actually laughing! Laughing! I couldn’t believe Sionna got her laughing like that. It made me feel sad that I hadn’t heard that sound in so long. It was gentle and deep, really heartfelt.
“On Halloween, we would watch from the bushes, and my brothers would tip those outhouses over!” She laughed some more.
“Why don’t we go to church anymore?” She asked me, like I was the parent, and she was the child.
“Yes, I do. That would be fine. Let’s go next Sunday. It’s been too long.”
If Nick stepped foot into the church, I’m sure a bolt of lightning would hit the place. … I laughed out loud.
Ma and I were just zoning out to the tube when there was a knock at the door. I walked over to open it, but it opened by itself. In walked Nick.

source_excerpts.excerpt: “Oh My God! It’s you Nick! I thought we would never see you again. Where have you been hiding?” Ma was so excited, I thought she might fall out of her chair.
“This one been treatin’ you all right?” He asked about me like I was her hired nurse’s aide.
Then she held out her hand to shake the hand of my brother; my brother who looked at her hand like it was attached to an alien being.
“I’m Sionna. You must be Nick. Nice to meet you, I’ve heard so much about you.” “Uh, yea, likewise,” he reluctantly shook her hand.
“No thanks. I can’t eat that crap. Sugar makes me feel bloated and sick.”
Nick was staring Sionna up and down, as if deciding what to make of her. “So, I hear you’re a university lady,” he said.

source_excerpts.excerpt: “Well, professor, I hope the best for ya. It’s tough out there, and, like I was tellin’ my kid brother here….sometimes all that school just doesn’t pay off. … it’s even harder for a woman to teach at that level, or so I’ve heard.”
“Well, Nick, I have to disagree with you there. As a matter of fact, in the last forty years, bachelor’s degrees earned by female students have risen from about 35 to 55 percent.
“Well, if you ask me,” Nick responded, “that might be one of the reasons this country is heading down the flippin’ tubes. The women in this country can’t stay home and take care of their kids.”
Inside I was jumping up and down, and yelling “that’s my girl!”  I was so proud of her at that moment. Nick was sitting there stunned. Never before had someone out-worded him.
“Sounds like you two have it all figured out, with help of course from Doctor Sionna.” His sarcasm floated around the room and permeated the air like foul gas.
as he walked through the threshold, he lightly grabbed my shirt and whispered, “take care of Ma”, as he pulled his face in close to mine.

source_excerpts.excerpt: So, just like A Tale of Two Cities, it was the best of times, and it was the worst of times. I was keeping a low profile.

Nick came around more to check on Ma, and he even started laying out her pills and bringing her treats to eat. … She began to trust Nick again; actually doting on him like she used to. Nick was in – and I was out.
If I wasn’t taking orders at the pizza joint, I was going to the library to research the Peloponnesian Wars, or Greek mythology
As I read about Athena, my mind drifted to Sionna. They were both Goddesses of intelligence, and skill; peaceful warriors born fully armored and ready for a battle.
Nick, on the other hand, was more like this God named Menoetius. He was described as “hubristic”, which means exceedingly prideful and impetuous to the very end.
Ma was really our touchstone at this point. If it weren’t for her, we may not ever see each other.

source_excerpts.excerpt: Sionna and I were talking about the universe one day. It was one of those days that turned out to be nice when you thought it was going to suck.
Then, out of the blue, a class cancelled due to teacher staffing issues, then the pizza joint was slow. I was free to just hang out for once, so I headed over to see my girlfriend.
She was writing something on the computer in her living room about similarities in the creation stories of different religions.

source_excerpts.excerpt: I am riding in the passenger seat of Sionna’s Toyota Civic hatchback. It was an old car, but functional always. Great gas mileage. The road wound up and down the soft hills of Eastern Wisconsin in a rhythmic calming way.

It looked like a postcard with the bright blue sky, a few cumulous clouds, pine trees, and maples showing their green leaves in the summer sun. It was absolutely beautiful and reminded me of the pictures I had seen of England’s pasturelands.

It was the first week of summer break, and I was actually taking a break for once. School was out for the week before summer session started, Ma was in the hands of Nick the chameleon, and we were on the way to visit Sionna’s parents, Mushroom and Ginger.
“They actually like being called Mushroom and Ginger? You’re sure?” I had a strange vision of walking up to two old hippies with smoke stringing out of their mouths, moccasins, the works…..

source_excerpts.excerpt: “Actually, my father’s parents had five other kids, so I think they just mentally divorced him. I do remember seeing them once at the farm. It was a bad scene with a lot of yelling and my grandmother crying.
I just remember her hair piled up in a beehive, and a cheap looking blue polyester dress. She looked at me through the screen door, tears streaming down her face.
“Mom’s parents tried really hard to be in our lives when I was little, but Grandma and Grandpa just didn’t get tofu.” She started to chuckle.
They also discouraged any belief in the tooth fairy, the Easter bunny, or Santa Claus. Elves….that was possible. Wood nymphs, the spiritual life of plants, trees, and water….all ok.
“So, your parents are pagans.” She looked at me and decidedly said, “Yea. I guess that’s as close as we’re going to get.”

This is Murphy’s Law, whatever you think is going to happen is not what happens. Basically, it’s pure irony. Like that song by Morissette, “It’s like a fly in your Chardonnay, it’s like rain on your wedding day….”
And that’s exactly what happened….everything went to shit. It’s such a long story, I guess I’ll just relive this nightmare.
I am a guy. What I mean by that is, I look like a guy, I act like a guy, and I think like a guy. … Then, when a woman enters your life, you start to see things from a different angle.
